entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the agricultural practices and pest management are discussed

entities.name: NATURAL RESOURCE MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The management of natural resources in the context of Indian agriculture

entities.name: CHEMICAL PESTICIDES
entities.description: Chemical substances used for pest control that have led to resistance and residues

entities.name: PESTS
entities.description: Organisms that cause damage to crops and have developed resistance to pesticides

entities.name: RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of pests to survive exposure to chemical pesticides

entities.name: PATHOGENS
entities.description: Disease-causing organisms that can emerge as secondary pests

entities.name: RESIDUES
entities.description: Chemical remains from pesticides that enter the food chain

entities.name: FOOD CHAIN
entities.description: The sequence of organisms through which pesticide residues occur

entities.name: INTEGRATED PEST MANAGEMENT
entities.description: A sustainable approach to pest control aimed at minimizing health damage and increasing profits

entities.name: INSECT PESTS
entities.description: Insects that cause economic damage to major crops

entities.name: DISEASES
entities.description: Pathological conditions affecting major crops of economic importance

entities.name: MAJOR CROPS
entities.description: Primary agricultural crops grown in India

entities.name: ECO-FRIENDLY MANAGEMENT STRATEGIES
entities.description: Sustainable strategies for managing insect pests and diseases

entities.name: WATERSHED PERSPECTIVE
entities.description: A spatial approach used for implementing eco-friendly management strategies
